mnemonics: First Order Inductive Learner: FOIL hunts for Rules like a treasure hunter finding First-Order gems, Inductively Learning patterns.
keyPoints: FOIL is an algorithm for learning first-order Horn clauses from relational data.
It uses a sequential covering algorithm to learn one rule at a time, removing covered positive examples after each iteration.
It employs a general-to-specific search, starting with an empty rule body and adding literals to specialize it, guided by information gain.
commonMistakes: Confusing FOIL (the algorithm) with the FOIL method for multiplying binomials in algebra.
Forgetting that FOIL learns Horn clauses, which have a head and a body, not arbitrary first-order logic formulas.
Incorrectly applying information gain by using the wrong counts for positive and negative examples covered by a candidate literal.
